findings.problem: The chapter's best image is Gloria's single nod, but the ending does not trust it. Once line 181 lands, lines 183-185 keep translating the gesture into abstract meaning ("acknowledgment," "the seeing was enough," "the thing itself") instead of letting the visible action and Doug's reaction carry the climax. That costs the ending shortlist-level control.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the interpretation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:183 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:185. Keep the local sequence around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:181 anchored in what Doug can see and physically register, then move him offstage without re-decoding the nod in essayistic language.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after the nod at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:181, the revised passage gives no more than one brief interpretive sentence before Doug exits and a cold reader can still tell that Gloria's response matters more to him than the applause. FAIL if the text still spends multiple abstract sentences explaining what the nod meant.

findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly grades Doug's set instead of letting the room grade it. Line 91 announces "This was the stretch that went on too long," and line 139 explains that the unevenness is "not a failure in it." Those verdicts convert dramatized dead air into commentary and create sprawl where the chapter should trust observable pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:91-95 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:139, cut the self-diagnosis and keep the room evidence: coughs, phone glow, partial recovery, the twelfth-row exchange, and Doug's choice to continue. Preserve the state shift, but let behavior rather than editorial summary establish it.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a cold reader can point to the exact moment Doug loses part of the room and the later fact of the set's uneven survival using only on-page signals in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:91-95 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:139, with no sentence that labels the stretch "too long" or says the unevenness is "not a failure." FAIL if the revision still grades the set after the room behavior has already shown the grade.

findings.problem: The chapter reproduces calibration-only style-bible example lines instead of generating fresh dialogue. The off-script opening at line 73 echoes Doug's banned "I had something prepared" calibration, line 109 reuses the "bought better cereal" beat almost verbatim, and line 123 lifts Roy's anti-video wording in the same sequence. The style bible explicitly marks those examples as do-not-copy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the speech at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:109, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123 so the factual beats remain (abandoned script, post-viral cereal upgrade, Roy refusing the video) but the wording, syntax, and cadence are new. Do not preserve the current signature phrases or Roy's current sentence order.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:109, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123 still convey those three facts but no longer contain recognizable carryover phrasing such as "I had something prepared," "bought better cereal," or Roy's current "I don't watch videos / I barely watch television / ... adds anything" sequence. FAIL if any of those calibration phrasings or their cadence remain recognizable.

findings.problem: The supposedly live, unprotected monologue still resolves too often into polished pull-quote language. "There's a sound a room makes when it's leaving" at line 93 and "Polite applause is someone filling out the paperwork on your failure while smiling at you" at line 105 sound composed in hindsight rather than spoken under pressure, which blunts the chapter's claim that the set has finally stopped being written.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the live speech at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:105, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123. Let Doug restart, qualify, or anchor the thought in immediate room or work detail before it resolves; preserve the insight, but make it feel found in the moment rather than minted as a quote.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the current quotable formulations in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:105, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123 are broken into messier live speech with self-interruption, revision, or concrete factual anchoring, while the reader still understands Doug's point about losing the room, polite applause, and Roy's nonjudgment. FAIL if the revised lines still read like standalone aphorisms.

findings.problem: Chapter-level prose scaffolding repeats well past the allowed threshold. "He talked about..." recurs across the set, bare "He paused." appears throughout the monologue, and the applause beat serially uses "He looked..." in three successive turns. The repeated templates make a supposedly unstable hour feel mechanically sequenced.
findings.rewrite_direction: Across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:83-109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:119-155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:167-179, replace most stock lead-ins with scene-specific transitions. Render some pauses through breath, gesture, silence, or audience behavior instead of the bare sentence, and vary the applause sightline tracking so the movement feels discovered rather than templated.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised chapter uses the bare templates "He paused.", "He talked about...", and serial "He looked..." no more than twice each, with each surviving instance doing distinct dramatic work in the named spans. FAIL if the monologue still advances through repeated stock cues instead of scene-specific movement.

summary: The chapter lands its required plot beats and stays consistent with continuity, tense, and the comic-cathartic premise, but it is not pass-ready. Calibration-line leakage from the style bible, over-explanatory narration around the set's unevenness and ending, polished-too-quotable off-script speech, and repeated sentence templates keep the performance from feeling fully original, lived, and formally controlled.
